title: ProtocolLab Component Package v2
purpose: Define the public component package contract that lets ProtocolLab compose versioned implementations, test executors, and scenario packs without baking protocol capability into hosted lab workers.
scope: This specification covers protocol-lab-package-v2 manifest identity, package kinds, dependency declarations, package-relative catalog entry manifests, package provenance, and package set compatibility. It does not define legacy package migration, untrusted sandboxing, package signing, package storage APIs, automatic dependency fetching, or private fleet scheduling policy.

requirements.title: V2 root manifest
requirements.statement: A v2 ProtocolLab component package MUST contain protocol-lab-package.json with schemaVersion protocol-lab-package-v2, packageId, packageVersion, kind, and entryManifests.

requirements.title: Package kinds
requirements.statement: The v2 manifest kind MUST be one of implementation, test-executor, scenario-pack, or toolchain.

requirements.title: Catalog entry overlays
requirements.statement: A v2 package MAY provide package-relative entry manifests for implementations, test executors, scenarios, and suites.

requirements.title: Capability dependency declarations
requirements.statement: A v2 component package MAY declare package dependencies or required capabilities as metadata, but the public package contract MUST NOT require executable entrypoints, local launch commands, or implementation-specific runtime prerequisites.

requirements.title: Component compatibility semantics
requirements.statement: A v2 component package MUST declare kind-specific provided component metadata: implementation packages declare providedImplementations, test-executor packages declare providedTestExecutors, scenario-pack packages declare providedSuites or providedScenarios, and toolchain packages declare dependencies.requiredCapabilities.

requirements.title: Run provenance
requirements.statement: Every hosted run result that uses component packages SHOULD record each resolved package's packageId, packageVersion, SHA-256, kind, effective manifest IDs, and selected component IDs.

requirements.title: No legacy v2 aliases
requirements.statement: A protocol-lab-package-v2 manifest MUST use kind test-executor and providedTestExecutors for tester packages.

requirements.title: Catalog overlay materialization
requirements.statement: Implementations that consume component packages MUST materialize package entry manifests into generated per-attempt catalog overlays without modifying the public contract repository.

requirements.title: Package-relative entry paths
requirements.statement: Entry manifest paths MUST be relative paths inside the package and must not be rooted paths, URI paths, or contain traversal segments.

requirements.title: Incompatible component rejection
requirements.statement: Implementations that consume component packages MUST reject incompatible component sets rather than substituting a different protocol lane.

requirements.title: No legacy package semantics
requirements.statement: New v2 docs, schemas, tooling, and hosted ingestion MUST NOT use load-generator, load-generator package kinds, or providedLoadGenerators as v2 package semantics.

requirements.title: Package ID ownership claims
requirements.statement: A packageId value MUST NOT be treated as proof of publisher, namespace, trademark, or package-index ownership.
requirements.rationale: Package identity is a stable contract key, but consumers need separate admission policy before trusting who controls a namespace.

requirements.title: Multi-publisher namespace admission
requirements.statement: A multi-publisher hosted lab or package index MUST enforce a namespace reservation or publisher-verification policy before admitting packages whose packageId uses a reserved, first-party, organizational, or otherwise confusing namespace.
requirements.rationale: Labs can stay lightweight while still rejecting package identities that imply an unverified owner or reserved public prefix.
